timeline.notes: SWEEP! Anthony sweeps from closed guard.
timeline.coaching_tip: Broken posture = swept. Stand up in closed guard.
Frame on neck and hip. Shrimp.
Trap and roll or elbow-knee escape.

timeline.notes: Round ends. Both players separate. Time called or mutual stop.

summary.session_overview: Roll 2 (continuation after tap). Greig and Anthony reset and continue. Anthony sweeps Greig from closed guard at 2:40 and spends the remainder on top, advancing through side control → north-south → mount. Greig shows resilience recovering to open guard at the end.

key_moments.description: Greig caught inside Anthony's closed guard.
key_moments.suggestion: STAND UP to break closed guard.

key_moments.description: Anthony sweeps Greig from closed guard.
key_moments.suggestion: Broken posture = swept. Stand up in closed guard every time.
Frame and spin when they transition.
Post-sweep recovery too slow. Frame and shrimp immediately.

top_3_improvements: Closed guard posture: Got swept because of broken posture. STAND UP to break guard.
Bottom recovery speed: After being swept, gave up positions too quickly. Frame and hip escape immediately.
Bottom guard retention: Once on bottom, need active guard with frames and foot placement.
strengths_observed: Resilience — fought to the end and recovered to open guard
Kept engaging despite being on bottom
summary.guard_retention_reason: Guard passed quickly after the sweep. Gave up side control → north-south → mount.
summary.positional_awareness_reason: Didn't recognise the sweep setup from closed guard. Slow recovery once on bottom.
summary.transition_quality_reason: All transitions were defensive and unsuccessful. Went from bottom side control to mount without effective resistance.
summary.overall_notes: The bottom game needs work. The sweep from closed guard is a recurring vulnerability — broken posture leads to the sweep every time. Practice standing guard breaks and bottom escapes.
